intro: Jacobaea thuretii, also known as Pyrethrum thuretii, is a perennial plant species belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow or white flowers. Traditionally, the plant has been used in herbal medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The bioactive constituents of Jacobaea thuretii include sesquiterpene lactones, such as pyrethrin I and II, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is often used to make herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, as well as extracts for topical applications, such as creams and ointments.
